skill.name: spawn-terminal
skill.description: Run non-AI CLI commands in a separate terminal after checking command help and options.
skill.summary: Open a separate terminal for raw CLI commands with a required help-check workflow.
skill.author: Consiliency

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly identified that this community skill is designed to run external CLI commands, which creates real misuse risk if a user supplies unsafe commands. The filesystem path traversal and weak cryptography blocker hits appear to be false positives from relative documentation paths and ordinary Markdown text. No evidence found of credential theft, network exfiltration, persistence, obfuscation, or prompt injection that targets the auditor.

medium_findings.title: Raw External Command Execution Capability
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill explicitly instructs an assistant to execute user-requested CLI commands in a new terminal. This is the intended function, but it can run destructive, networked, or data-accessing commands if the user request is unsafe.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files repeatedly describe running raw CLI commands and calling fork_terminal. The risk is semantic and direct, although it matches the documented purpose rather than hidden malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Relative Path Traversal Static Hits Are Documentation References
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The path traversal detections point to relative references for reading a sibling helper file and locating the helper function. No evidence found that the skill constructs arbitrary filesystem paths or reads user-controlled paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only evidence is documented relative paths, not executable path traversal logic. Confidence that this is a real security issue is low.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The cited lines contain the skill description and a Markdown table header, not cryptographic code or algorithms. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, insecure ciphers, or any cryptographic operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found no cryptography-related content. Confidence that this represents a real weak cryptography issue is very low.

dangerous_patterns.title: External CLI Command Dispatch
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs the assistant to pass commands to fork_terminal after a help check. This pattern can execute arbitrary local commands and should be used only with trusted user intent.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is documented across the main skill file and cookbook. The danger comes from command execution capability, not from concealed malicious code.

content.user_title: Run CLI Commands in Separate Terminals
content.value_statement: Developers often need an assistant to launch long-running or interactive CLI tools without blocking the main conversation. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured workflow for checking help output before opening a separate terminal.
actual_capabilities: Identifies when a request is for a non-AI CLI command.
Requires a help check before running the requested command.
Launches commands through the fork_terminal helper.
Supports optional output capture for blocking command runs.
Supports optional debug logging to a file.
Redirects AI agent requests to a separate agent-spawning skill.
limitations: It can execute unsafe commands if the user request is unsafe.
It depends on the fork_terminal helper being available in the expected path.
It does not validate command safety beyond checking help output.
It is not intended for spawning Claude, Codex, or other AI agents.

use_cases.title: Run Build Tools
use_cases.description: Launch npm, Python, or other build commands in a separate terminal while keeping the main assistant session available.

use_cases.title: Process Media Files
use_cases.description: Start tools such as ffmpeg after reviewing available options and expected command syntax.

use_cases.title: Inspect CLI Behavior
use_cases.description: Check command help output before running a tool with user-selected arguments.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Simple Command
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a known local command.
prompt_templates.prompt: Open a new terminal and run npm run build after checking the command help output.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Media Conversion
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a command with input and output files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Spawn a terminal for ffmpeg to convert video.mp4 into output.gif. Check ffmpeg help first.

prompt_templates.title: Capture Command Output
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request where the assistant should wait for results.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run python script.py in a separate terminal and capture the output so I can review the result here.

prompt_templates.title: Debug a CLI Invocation
prompt_templates.prompt: Launch the requested CLI command with debug logging enabled, then tell me where the output log was written.

output_examples.input: Open a terminal and run npm run build.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks the npm command help, opens a new terminal, and starts the build command.

output_examples.input: Run ffmpeg to convert a video to a GIF.
output_examples.output: The assistant reviews ffmpeg help, then launches the conversion command in a separate terminal.

output_examples.input: Run my Python script and capture the output.
output_examples.output: The assistant runs the Python command with capture enabled and returns the completed command output.

best_practices: Review the exact command and arguments before launching a terminal.
Use output capture only when the command should finish quickly.
Prefer debug logging for long-running commands that may need later inspection.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for AI agent spawning requests.
Do not run destructive commands without explicit user confirmation.
Do not skip the command help check for unfamiliar tools.

faq.question: Does this skill execute local commands?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed to launch non-AI CLI commands in a separate terminal.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for untrusted commands?
faq.answer: No. Users should inspect commands before execution, especially commands that modify files or use the network.

faq.question: Can it run Claude, Codex, or Claude Code agents?
faq.answer: No. The skill says AI agent requests should use a separate agent-spawning skill.

faq.question: Why does it check help output first?
faq.answer: The help check reduces syntax mistakes and helps the assistant understand available options before running the command.

faq.question: Can it capture command output?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented helper supports blocking output capture when capture mode is enabled.

faq.question: Can it write debug logs?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented helper supports writing full terminal output to a debug file.
